summary: Ensure the app-rendered close button runs native terminal shutdown before closing the Tauri window.

review_items.summary: The Tauri host exposes a terminal_close_all command that drains every active terminal instance from the shared terminal map, emits close progress events, and runs existing terminal cleanup sequences concurrently before returning.
The app-rendered close button shows a blocking closing overlay, awaits terminal_close_all, displays terminal close progress, and then calls the Tauri window close API.
The desktop shell displays a fullscreen closing workspace overlay with a loader and closed terminal count while terminal cleanup is running.
Desktop auth, billing, workspace API, and database contracts are unchanged.

rules: Application close from the custom title bar must trigger the same native terminal process cleanup path used by explicit terminal close controls before closing the window.
The first close click must immediately show a fullscreen closing workspace overlay so terminal cleanup cannot look like an ignored click.
The closing overlay must show how many active terminal cleanup sequences have completed out of the total being closed.
The app should automatically close when terminal cleanup reaches the final count or terminal_close_all returns.
Closing all terminals must drain the active terminal map before process cleanup so late resize/write calls cannot keep stale PTY handles alive.
Closing all active terminals during app shutdown should run bounded per-terminal cleanup concurrently so multi-terminal workspaces are not delayed by serial child-process polling.
The window close action should continue closing the app even if terminal shutdown reports a best-effort failure.
This change must not alter desktop auth, billing, workspace API, or database behavior.

compatibility.notes: Adds a desktop-only Tauri command and changes the local close-button sequence; no hosted API or data contract changes.

tests.summary: Validate the React WebView close-button change.
Validate the Tauri terminal close-all command.
